# Course plan

## Preparatory phase
### Afternoon session 1 (20.04.2026)

- Course logistics, programme, learning objectives 
- Introduction to summer droughts in Switzerland, drought monitoring 
- Identification of essential variables 
- Starting points for data download, analysis, and interpretation. Analysis should put current state into a multi-year reference 
- Approach to collecting stakeholder perspectives, guiding field visits 

### Afternoon session 2 (11.05.2026)

- Support for data download, analysis, and interpretation 
- Consolidate questions stakeholders to a common set 
- Preparation/guidance for presentation (structure) 

## Field phase

| Date      | Day | Morning                                             | Afternoon                                      |
|-----------|-----|-----------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------|
| 8.6.      | Mo  | Presentation and discussion<br>Weather outlook       | Stadt Bern (Rundgang)                          |
| 9.6.      | Di  | Swisstopo                                           | BAFU                                           |
| 10.6.     | Mi  | DOK Trial Therwil                                   | DOK Trial Therwil                              |
| 11.6.     | Do  | Kanton Freiburg                                     | MeteoSuisse, Payerne                           |
| 12.6.     | Fr  | Drought experiment (Biology, Uni Bern)              | Burgergemeinde Bern                            |
| 14.6.     | So  |                                                     | Travel to Wallis                               |
| 15.6.     | Mo  | Integrales Wasserressourcenmanagement, Naters       | Integrales Wasserressourcenmanagement, Naters<br>Fireside input |
| 16.6.     | Di  | Drought experiment Pfynwald (WSL)                   | Wildfire Leuk<br>Return to Bern                |
